---
title: "Steve Haigh Haigh"
subtitle: "Biomedical Sciences M.Res · University of Reading"
about:
  template: trestles
  # image: profile.jpg  # uncomment once you drop a square photo in the repo root
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/stevehaigh
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/shaigh
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:steve_a_haigh@hotmail.com
---

Software developer of 30 years, now researching biomedical sciences at the University of Reading. My dissertation extends the *wcEcoli* whole-cell modelling framework to build the first computational model of a human platelet, supervised by Dr Mike Fry.

This site collects notes, slide decks, and project write-ups from the intersection of software and wet-lab biology.

## Currently working on

- Whole-cell modelling of human platelet activation
- Containerised simulation pipelines and Dash-based exploration UIs
- Bridging classical software engineering practice with computational biology workflows

## Elsewhere

See the [Projects](projects.qmd) page for repos and decks, or the [Blog](blog.qmd) for longer-form notes.
